creator: stefan@FreeBSD.org
text:
For Dirk and for the record - reply received by submitter:

a d, 01.05.13, 10:56h CEST:

> 
> 
> Hi Stefan,
> 
> 
> 
> Im really happy with most aspects of ezjail, its a great utility. Its just this
> one issue that affects us from having a tidy/consistent configuration.
> 
> Currently the code that is used to sanitise the jail name is restrictive
> preventing a separation character from being used in the name to easily distinguish
> between the host server name and the jail.
> 
> 
> 
> We would like to be able to name the jail directory, the jail name and
> the jails host name consistently.
> (Our naming convention defines using a '-' character to distinguish network interface names on a server)eg.
> 
> export jail_server-j1_hostname="server-j1.domain.local"
> 
> export jail_server-j1_rootdir="/jails/server-j1"
> 
> 
> 
> Thanks
> 
> Adam
